rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation while preserving the AADL architecture structure.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers and types present in the analyzed AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component ports or implementation bindings.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not part of the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or component port references in implementation scopes without proper variable representation.
Do not assume that AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: Model analyst tasks and AADL structural extraction
